name: --verbose
-v
options.description: Increases the logging verbosity

name: --init
-i
options.description: Writes the default configuration file to cliff.toml

name: --latest
-l
options.description: Processes the commits starting from the latest tag

name: --current
options.description: Processes the commits that belong to the current tag

name: --unreleased
-u
options.description: Processes the commits that do not belong to a tag

name: --date-order
options.description: Sorts the tags chronologically

name: --context
options.description: Prints changelog context as JSON

name: --help
-h
options.description: Prints help information

name: --version
-V
options.description: Prints version information

name: --config
-c
options.description: Sets the configuration file

name: --workdir
-w
options.description: Sets the working directory

name: --repository
-r
options.description: Sets the git repository

name: --include-path
options.description: Sets the path to include related commits

name: --exclude-path
options.description: Sets the path to exclude related commits

name: --with-commit
options.description: Sets custom commit messages to include in the changelog

name: --prepend
-p
options.description: Prepends entries to the given changelog file

name: --output
-o
options.description: Writes output to the given file

name: --tag
-t
options.description: Sets the tag for the latest version

name: --body
-b
options.description: Sets the template for the changelog body

name: --strip
-s
options.description: Strips the given parts from the changelog

name: --sort
options.description: Sets sorting of the commits inside sections

args.name: range
args.description: Sets the commit range to process
